pairs.before: ensures end to end functionality
pairs.critique_verbatim: I dunno if ensures end to end functionality is meaningful as a phrase; you can instead talk about whether you improved code coverage, or made the edge case testing more robust or something
pairs.after: Talk about whether you improved code coverage or made edge case testing more robust

pairs.before: improving app functionality and delivering a seamless, consistent user experience across device
pairs.critique_verbatim: Your actual work is very vague and the focus is just on listing tech stacks. For example, your first bullet spends 2 whole lines to just tell me you used React Native. "improving app functionality and delivering a seamless, consistent user experience across device" isn't specific enough to tell me anything about what you actually did
pairs.after: Provide specific details about what you actually did

pairs.before: more accurate job matches
pairs.critique_verbatim: What are "more accurate job matches"? Like the rank-match kind of match? You're describing what the tool should ideally do (enhance UX, optimize job search), but you haven't told me enough of what it does to believe you
pairs.after: Clarify what more accurate job matches mean and what the tool actually does

pairs.before: Achieved measurable performance improvement
pairs.critique_verbatim: "Achieved measurable performance improvement" - This is the perfect place to give me some stats
pairs.after: Provide specific statistics to support the claim

pairs.before: Wrote a recursive equation evaluation algorithm to compare function values with points on the graph
pairs.critique_verbatim: You could also expand on "Wrote a recursive equation evaluation algorithm to compare function values with points on the graph". I assume this is to calculate how to colour different points on the graph? This project could be a good differentiator since generic full stack/discord bot projects are common, so give it more focus if you can
pairs.after: Expand on the project to clarify its purpose and contribution

pairs.before: Lightweight but delicated React
pairs.critique_verbatim: Couple spelling issues I saw. "Lightweight but delicated React" delicate?
pairs.after: Correct spelling to 'Lightweight but delicate React'

pairs.before: __candidate for __ honours bachelor's of X
pairs.critique_verbatim: i normally put it as "__candidate for __ honours bachelor's of X" - would help to fill out the space a bit more
pairs.after: Format degree as '__candidate for __ honours bachelor's of X'

pairs.before: Its also nice to simplify down some sentences; your line about improving test performance by 80% somewhat repeats the opening of that line (optimized test performance), and this can be shortened by just opening with “implemented parallel execution utilizing multithreading …”
pairs.critique_verbatim: implemented parallel execution utilizing multithreading …
pairs.after: Begin with 'implemented parallel execution utilizing multithreading …' to simplify the sentence.

pairs.before: Executed competitive analysis
pairs.critique_verbatim: Executed competitive analysis
pairs.after: Clarify the meaning of 'executed competitive analysis'.

pairs.before: focused on CI/CD practices
pairs.critique_verbatim: focused on CI/CD practices
pairs.after: Avoid using fluff words that don't convey meaningful information.

pairs.before: Handled the previous bugs and issues related to database
pairs.critique_verbatim: Handled the previous bugs and issues related to database
pairs.after: Provide more details to clarify the scope of handling bugs and issues related to the database.

pairs.before: Developed full data **ingestion** pipeline for the CanDIG federated cancer research database using __________, allowing for researchers to upload partient data for the first time.
pairs.critique_verbatim: Developed full data **ingestion** pipeline for the CanDIG federated cancer research database using __________, allowing for researchers to upload partient data for the first time.
pairs.after: Ensure that both bullet points belong to the same sentence for fluidity.

pairs.before: developed the backend
pairs.critique_verbatim: developed the backend
pairs.after: Provide more detail by specifying what was developed, such as 'developed user registration system?' or 'developed emailer?'.

pairs.before: Created over X articles on company website by developing templates using ....
pairs.critique_verbatim: Created over X articles on company website by developing templates using ....
pairs.after: Rephrase to show quantifiable impact, such as 'Created over X articles on company website by developing templates using ....'.

pairs.before: Web stuff is featured prominently
pairs.critique_verbatim: cut that down
pairs.after: Cut down the prominence of web-related content.

pairs.before: 'utilized the atlassian API along with'
pairs.critique_verbatim: is so much fluff. Start your sentences/bullets with, what did you build, followed by the tech
pairs.after: Start sentences/bullets with what you built, followed by the technology used.

pairs.before: most bigger/reasonable tech internships we give a dedicated project for the intern to own from start to finish
pairs.critique_verbatim: so this is more of the norm
pairs.after: Highlight that most internships provide a dedicated project for the intern to own from start to finish.

pairs.before: it's good rn but non technical ppl reading it would just find it too wordy
pairs.critique_verbatim: for a technical person
pairs.after: Simplify wording to ensure clarity for non-technical readers.

pairs.before: if u are gonna use 2 line bullets make sure u use the whole second line
pairs.critique_verbatim: bc otherwise it's just wasted space that could be used better
pairs.after: Ensure to use the entire second line when using two-line bullets to utilize space effectively.

pairs.before: 60% company wide productivity increase
pairs.critique_verbatim: doesn't seem feasible for an intern to do (maybe u did do it but as a reader it seems dubious)
pairs.after: Reconsider presenting the 60% productivity increase as it may seem dubious for an intern.

pairs.before: I have no idea what you did at Connexix
pairs.critique_verbatim: Looks like you implemented some things you learnt in school. No idea why those were used or what problems they were solving though.
pairs.after: Clarify your contributions and the problems addressed during your time at Connexix.

pairs.before: No one 'architects' tests
pairs.critique_verbatim: just say you wrote 100+ tests
pairs.after: State that you wrote 100+ tests instead of using the term 'architects'.

pairs.before: The first job is p reasonable
pairs.critique_verbatim: but just reads a lil clunky. You’re stuffing a lot of impactful but a teeny bit generic info there.
pairs.after: Revise the description of the first job to improve clarity and narrative coherence.

pairs.before: there’s a lot of types of things you worked on that you’re writing as either an afterthought or you’re bundling with not like items
pairs.critique_verbatim: Framing matters here, so think about what narrative you want to present and it will come naturally to you what you should say.
pairs.after: Improve framing of diverse experiences to present a cohesive narrative.

pairs.before: I see a hint of these in yours, but it’s a little jumbled
pairs.critique_verbatim: Every resume tells a narrative about the person. What their history is, what their background is, but also what interests them and drives them.
pairs.after: Ensure your resume clearly communicates your personal narrative, interests, and motivations.

pairs.before: You wrote SQL for this, not SQL scripts
pairs.critique_verbatim: People are familiar with ETLs, but 'SQL scripts' probably undersells what you actually did.
pairs.after: Specify that you wrote SQL instead of referencing it as 'SQL scripts'.

pairs.before: increased accuracy by 30%
pairs.critique_verbatim: increased accuracy by 30% - is this additive or multiplicative? unsure
pairs.after: Clarify if the 30% increase in accuracy is additive or multiplicative.

pairs.before: for mathsoc director, finance
pairs.critique_verbatim: we could expand on the bullet points here - how many requests did you audit, what was involved in cash closing procedures.
pairs.after: Expand on the bullet points by adding how many requests were audited and details about cash closing procedures.

pairs.before: for the competitions/conferences:
pairs.critique_verbatim: the cumc talk is really cool - we could consider adding how many people you presented to? or where it was physically? mention that it's a national conference?
pairs.after: Include the number of attendees and location of the cumc talk, and mention that it was a national conference.

pairs.before: You have really good projects, the formatting things i said above apply here i.e. bolding and quantifying.
pairs.critique_verbatim: You have really good projects, the formatting things i said above apply here i.e. bolding and quantifying.
pairs.after: Apply bolding and quantifying to the project descriptions.

pairs.before: For example, here you bolded a metric ($300) and some libraries (MappedIn, MERN)
pairs.critique_verbatim: For example, here you bolded a metric ($300) and some libraries (MappedIn, MERN), however you didnt bold Express.js and MongoDB, which id argue is just as important or more important than MappedIn.
pairs.after: Bold all key technologies and metrics for consistency in emphasis.
